metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the credential storage and injection mechanism for the Gerrit HTTP-password (e.g., `REBAR_GERRIT_VOTER_PASSWORD` env var, AWS SSM/Secrets Manager, mounted Kubernetes secret) and explicitly prohibit storing it in plaintext in `rebar.toml` or any version-controlled file — add this to the `[gerrit_voter]` config schema and the ADR 0013.
findings.finding: The plan describes using a Gerrit service-account HTTP password in HTTP Basic Auth ("username + generated HTTP password, base64-encoded in the Authorization: Basic … header") but never specifies how or where this credential is stored and injected at runtime. The `[gerrit_voter]` config section in `rebar.toml` is described in detail (enabled, enabled_projects, max_vote, label, checkout_root) but the credential fields are entirely absent — leaving open the possibility that the password lands in a plaintext config file or is hard-coded.
findings.location: Gerrit REST API surface section — Auth subsection; Staged opt-in + rollback section (`[gerrit_voter]` config schema)
findings.suggested_fix: Add `gerrit_voter.password_env_var` (default `REBAR_GERRIT_VOTER_HTTP_PASSWORD`) to the config schema; document that the credential MUST be injected via environment variable or a secrets manager (not stored in `rebar.toml`); add a startup assertion that rejects a config where the password appears to be a literal string in the TOML file. Reference this in ADR 0013 under the auth section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a concurrency-safety strategy for the per-project checkout (e.g., per-project file lock, one-queue-per-project serialization, or ephemeral clone-per-invocation) so concurrent webhook deliveries for the same project cannot race on the shared checkout directory.
findings.finding: The per-project local checkout directory under `gerrit_voter.checkout_root` is mutated (git fetch) by the patchset handler on every event, but the plan specifies no concurrency control for concurrent webhook deliveries targeting the same project checkout simultaneously.
findings.suggested_fix: The simplest safe strategy is ephemeral clone-per-invocation (clone to a temp dir, review, discard) — avoids all shared mutable state at the cost of more disk I/O. Alternatively, a per-project `fcntl.flock` on a sentinel file in `checkout_root/{project}` serializes concurrent fetches with minimal complexity. Document the chosen strategy in the 'Event delivery + repo_root acquisition' section and in ADR 0013.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Address the concurrent-patchset-event race for the shared-checkout model: either specify a per-project advisory lock (e.g. a `.git`-adjacent lockfile held for the fetch+diff window) to serialize concurrent handlers, or adopt a per-invocation ephemeral worktree / temp clone so each handler works in isolation — and document the chosen approach in the patchset-handler spec and ADR 0013.
findings.finding: The plan's per-project shared-checkout model has an unaddressed concurrent-event race: multiple near-simultaneous patchset-created webhooks for the same Gerrit project all fetch their respective refs into the same `checkout_root/{project}` directory; git fetch is not safe to run concurrently against the same .git repo, and the diff read from the working tree can interleave with a concurrent fetch, producing a diff for the wrong ref.
findings.location: Section 'Event delivery + repo_root acquisition' / repo_root paragraph

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete mechanism for force-enabling the gate in the voter context: since `LLMConfig` has no `enable_code_review` field and `code_review_enabled(repo_root)` reads the file at `repo_root` independently of `cfg`, the adapter must use a different override path — e.g. set `REBAR_VERIFY_ENABLE_CODE_REVIEW=true` in the process environment before calling `produce_code_review_verdict`, write a temporary in-memory config override (the `cli_overrides` path in `load_config`), or add a direct `enabled_override` parameter to `produce_code_review_verdict`; the plan must name the exact mechanism so the AC8 test is implementable.
findings.finding: The plan's force-enable override mechanism is mechanically incorrect: it states the adapter 'passes an LLMConfig/override with enable_code_review=True', but LLMConfig (llm/config.py:461) has no enable_code_review field — the gate's enable check (gate_dispatch.py:422) calls code_review_enabled(repo_root), which reads verify.enable_code_review from the rebar.toml at repo_root entirely independently of the cfg object. An LLMConfig cannot carry this flag, so the described override path does not work and the implementer will need a different mechanism.
findings.location: Section 'The adapter seam (the d251-S4b contract)' / gate-enable override paragraph; AC8
findings.suggested_fix: The cleanest codebase-aligned approach: have the adapter call `produce_code_review_verdict` with a `repo_root` pointing to a temp directory that contains a minimal `rebar.toml` with `[verify]\nenable_code_review = true`, OR add an `enable_override: bool = False` parameter to `produce_code_review_verdict` that bypasses the `code_review_enabled` check when set — the latter requires a one-line change to gate_dispatch.py and is the most explicit contract. Alternatively, the env var `REBAR_VERIFY_ENABLE_CODE_REVIEW=true` can be set for the duration of the adapter call (thread-safe only if using a ContextVar, not os.environ).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the force-enable override mechanism: `LLMConfig` has no `enable_code_review` field (confirmed at `src/rebar/llm/config.py:461`), so specify the actual approach — e.g. add an `enable_code_review: bool | None = None` field to `LLMConfig` and thread it through `produce_code_review_verdict`/`code_review_enabled`, or add a dedicated `force_enable: bool = False` parameter to `produce_code_review_verdict`, or use `unittest.mock.patch` in tests — and update the plan accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the adapter forces the gate enabled by 'passing an LLMConfig/override with enable_code_review=True', but LLMConfig (src/rebar/llm/config.py:461–481) has no `enable_code_review` field. The gate-enable check in `produce_code_review_verdict` (gate_dispatch.py:422) calls `code_review_enabled(repo_root)` which reads `load_config(repo_root).verify.enable_code_review` — a path that goes through rebar.toml config, not through the `cfg: LLMConfig` argument. The described override mechanism does not match the actual code structure.
findings.location: The adapter seam (the d251-S4b contract) section — 'forces the gate enabled (passes an LLMConfig/override with enable_code_review=True)'; AC8
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `force_enable_code_review: bool = False` parameter to `produce_code_review_verdict` (alongside `cfg`) that, when True, bypasses the `code_review_enabled(repo_root)` check. The adapter passes `force_enable_code_review=True` when invoked by the voter. This is a single-line addition to gate_dispatch.py and matches the existing pattern of explicit override kwargs in that module.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the `LLMConfig/override with enable_code_review=True` force-enable description with the correct mechanism: the adapter must bypass or override `code_review_enabled()` directly (e.g. by monkeypatching it, using `REBAR_VERIFY_ENABLE_CODE_REVIEW`, or passing `cli_overrides={'verify': {'enable_code_review': True}}` to `load_config`) — `LLMConfig` has no `enable_code_review` field and cannot carry this override.
findings.finding: The plan's described force-enable mechanism is technically impossible as stated: it says the adapter 'passes an LLMConfig/override with enable_code_review=True', but `LLMConfig` (src/rebar/llm/config.py:461) has no `enable_code_review` field. That flag lives in `rebar.config.Config.verify.enable_code_review`, read by `code_review_enabled(repo_root)` via `load_config(repo_root)`. An `LLMConfig` cannot carry or override it. If the adapter naively passes its `LLMConfig` to `produce_code_review_verdict`, the gate-enable check will still consult `load_config(repo_root)` and return `False` for any reviewed repo without `enable_code_review=true` in its `rebar.toml` — defeating the stated override.
findings.location: Section: 'Gate-enable override (critical)'; AC8: 'The adapter forces enable_code_review=True when the voter invokes it'
findings.suggested_fix: The simplest correct approach: the adapter temporarily sets `REBAR_VERIFY_ENABLE_CODE_REVIEW=1` in the environment before calling `produce_code_review_verdict`, or the plan should document that the adapter calls `load_config` with explicit `cli_overrides={'verify': {'enable_code_review': True}}` and passes the resulting config-aware `repo_root=None` to sidestep the per-repo check. Alternatively, factor out a `code_review_enabled` parameter into `produce_code_review_verdict` so the adapter can pass `True` directly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the force-enable override mechanism: replace 'passes an LLMConfig/override with enable_code_review=True' with the actual approach — e.g., the adapter bypasses `code_review_enabled()` by calling `produce_code_review_verdict` with a synthetic `repo_root` containing `enable_code_review=true`, or by calling the env override `REBAR_VERIFY_ENABLE_CODE_REVIEW=1`, or by passing a pre-patched config object; specify which mechanism is authoritative so AC8's test is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The force-enable override is described as 'passes an LLMConfig/override with enable_code_review=True', but `LLMConfig` (confirmed at `src/rebar/llm/config.py:461-481`) has no `enable_code_review` field — that flag lives on `VerifyConfig` (`src/rebar/config.py:284`). The actual gate is `code_review_enabled(repo_root)` which reads `config.verify.enable_code_review`. An implementor following the plan's named mechanism literally cannot construct the specified override via `LLMConfig`.
findings.suggested_fix: Since `code_review_enabled(repo_root)` reads `config.load_config(repo_root).verify.enable_code_review`, the adapter can pass a temp `repo_root` with a synthetic `rebar.toml` containing `[verify]\nenable_code_review = true`, or monkeypatch `gate_dispatch.code_review_enabled` in the adapter layer. The plan should name one authoritative approach and update the AC8 test description accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add 'diff-unavailable' to AC5's enumeration of coverage-gap sub-reasons so the MESSAGE_TAGS test asserts all four rows (gate-disabled, llm-unavailable, scanner, diff-unavailable).
findings.finding: AC5 enumerates only three coverage-gap sub-reasons for its test assertion ('gate-disabled / llm-unavailable / scanner') but the plan body defines a fourth — 'diff-unavailable' — introduced in the 'Gerrit REST API surface' diff-fetch-failure section and AC7. The test specified in AC5 will not assert the full tag vocabulary documented in the body.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5; Review-message vocabulary section; AC7
findings.suggested_fix: In AC5, change 'gate-disabled / llm-unavailable / scanner' to 'gate-disabled / llm-unavailable / scanner / diff-unavailable'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `diff-unavailable` to AC5's enumerated coverage-gap sub-reason list so the `MESSAGE_TAGS` constant test is required to assert all four sub-reasons: `gate-disabled`, `llm-unavailable`, `scanner`, and `diff-unavailable`.
findings.finding: AC5's test-assertion requirement omits `diff-unavailable` from its coverage-gap sub-reason list, even though the plan body defines it as a fourth distinct sub-reason in both the 'Review-message vocabulary' and 'Gerrit REST API surface' sections. The AC reads 'gate-disabled / llm-unavailable / scanner' with no mention of `diff-unavailable`.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC5; Review-message vocabulary section
findings.suggested_fix: In AC5, replace 'gate-disabled / llm-unavailable / scanner' with 'gate-disabled / llm-unavailable / scanner / diff-unavailable'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'coverage-gap robot comment' in the 'REST vote failure behavior' section with 'coverage-gap review message (via `ReviewInput.message`)' to align with the explicit API surface design choice.
findings.finding: The 'REST vote failure behavior' section uses the term 'coverage-gap robot comment' to describe the INDETERMINATE-verdict message, but the 'Gerrit REST API surface' section explicitly states the message MUST be posted via `ReviewInput.message` and NOT via Gerrit's formal `robot_comments` field. An implementer reading the failure section in isolation could implement a `robot_comments` payload for the INDETERMINATE case, contradicting the deliberate design choice.
findings.location: REST vote failure behavior section — final bullet: 'decision=BLOCK ⇒ -1 + a coverage-gap robot comment (above)'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the final bullet of 'REST vote failure behavior' from 'decision=BLOCK ⇒ -1 + a coverage-gap robot comment (above)' to 'decision=BLOCK ⇒ -1 + a coverage-gap review message (via ReviewInput.message, tagged per the vocabulary above)'.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a RETIRE/cleanup path for per-project checkout directories (e.g., document that removing a project from `enabled_projects` SHOULD be accompanied by deleting `checkout_root/{project}`, add an operator note to the ADR 0013, or specify a periodic GC policy).
findings.finding: No RETIRE/cleanup path is specified for per-project checkout directories under `checkout_root`; retired or renamed projects leave stale directories on disk indefinitely.
findings.location: Event delivery + `repo_root` acquisition section; Staged opt-in + rollback section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the 'Staged opt-in + rollback' section: 'Retiring a project from `enabled_projects` SHOULD be accompanied by deleting `checkout_root/{project}` to reclaim disk; an operator runbook step covers this.' Also capture in ADR 0013.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align terminology in the 'REST vote failure behavior' section: replace 'service-account token' with 'service account HTTP-password credential' to match the auth description in the 'Gerrit REST API surface' section.
findings.finding: The 'REST vote failure behavior' section refers to the service account credential as 'service-account token' while the 'Gerrit REST API surface' section (and the broader plan) consistently calls it an 'HTTP-password credential' / 'HTTP password'. This is a minor terminology drift that could cause confusion about which credential type is used.
findings.location: REST vote failure behavior section; Gerrit REST API surface section — Auth subsection
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'urllib + Basic Auth (service-account token provisioned by d251)' to 'urllib + Basic Auth (service account HTTP-password credential provisioned by d251)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC or success note anchoring the value to a named stakeholder and a concrete (even if deferred) validation signal — e.g., name the persona ('platform engineers / Gerrit reviewers'), state the before-state ('changes merge without LLM review'), and tie WS6-a's closure to a specific observable in WS6-b (or add a minimal smoke criterion like 'running the handler against a local Gerrit container posts the expected vote').
findings.finding: No concrete validation mechanism exists within WS6-a itself: all ACs are satisfied by offline stub tests, and the only live/dogfood signal (observable improvement to a named stakeholder) is explicitly deferred to WS6-b with no before/after metric, SLO, or operational signal anchoring the value of THIS story.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
